title: 1940 United States census, Mike Clemans household in St. Paul
repository: National Archives and Records Administration, Record Group 29; accessed through Ancestry
citation: 1940 U.S. census, St. Paul, Ramsey County, Minnesota, enumeration district 90-297, sheet 15B, household 313, NARA microfilm T627, roll 2004; Ancestry collection 2442, record 98659217.
transcription: Mike Clemans, 37, born in Wisconsin, was enumerated with his wife and two daughters. The elder daughter was 18, making her too young to be the mother of a child born in January 1935. The record itself supplies no Talmage alias and does not establish identity with the open target.

title: Search for a 1921 St. Louis Clemans-Flaherty marriage
repository: Missouri marriage records accessed through Ancestry
citation: Negative search on 8 July 2026 in Ancestry, Missouri, U.S., Marriage Records, 1805-2002, collection 1171, for Clemans and Flaherty variants and a 16 April 1921 St. Louis marriage; no matching entry located.
transcription: Five name, spouse, date, and place variants returned no matching Clemans-Flaherty marriage in the named collection. The search did not obtain the groom's legal given name and may reflect a St. Louis City coverage gap.

title: 1930 United States census, Mike Clemens at Minnesota State Prison
repository: National Archives and Records Administration, Record Group 29; accessed through Ancestry
citation: 1930 U.S. census, Bayport, Washington County, Minnesota, Minnesota State Prison, enumeration district 4, sheet 7A, FHL microfilm 2340869; Ancestry collection 6224, record 78141306.
transcription: Mike Clemens, 26, born in Wisconsin, married, was enumerated as an inmate at Minnesota State Prison. The schedule gives age 17 at first marriage and parental birthplaces of Indiana and Wisconsin. It does not identify a spouse or establish that this man belongs to the target lineage.

title: 1905 Kansas state census, John F. Rust household
repository: Kansas State Historical Society; accessed through Ancestry
citation: 1905 Kansas state census, Martin Township, Smith County, Kansas, 1 March 1905, roll KS1905_158, line 8, family 2, household of J. F. Rust; Ancestry collection 1088, record 2007054.
transcription: J. F. Rust, 34, and E. B. Rust, 28, were enumerated with three children aged 10, 8, and 7. The daughter was recorded as Francis. No fourth child appears in this household.

title: 1900 United States census, John F. Rust household
repository: National Archives and Records Administration, Record Group 29; accessed through Ancestry
citation: 1900 U.S. census, Martin Township, Smith County, Kansas, enumeration district 162, sheet 9A and continuation 9B, dwelling 162, family 163, NARA microfilm T623, roll 501; Ancestry collection 7602, record 4888894.
transcription: John F. Rust, born April 1870 in Illinois, and wife Ettie, born March 1876 in Pennsylvania, were enumerated with three children. Ettie's children-born and children-living columns both read three. The daughter was recorded as Francis.

title: 1910 United States census, Rollie Clemens household in Barron County
repository: National Archives and Records Administration, Record Group 29; accessed through Ancestry
citation: 1910 U.S. census, Rice Lake Ward 4, Barron County, Wisconsin, enumeration district 19, sheet 11A, NARA microfilm T624, roll 1701, household of Rollie Clemens; Ancestry collection 7884, record 30554673.
transcription: Rollie Clemens, 30, and Ellen C. Clemens, 26, were enumerated with three children, including a seven-year-old son indexed with the given names Dewitt Talmadge. The record provides a same-name-family lead only; chronology later excludes its elder daughter from the target maternal role.

title: Wybelsum parish burial and marriage entries for the Rust family
repository: Evangelisch-Reformierte Kirche Wybelsum, Kreis Emden, reference 191/1; accessed through Ancestry
citation: Evangelisch-Reformierte Kirche Wybelsum, register Wybelsum und Wymeer, burials, entry 9, Jan Aissen Rust, died 23 June and buried 27 June 1830; marriages, pages 6-7, entry 11, Aisse Jansen Rust and Martha Jansen Haan, 4 December 1841. Ancestry collection 61229, records 12268901 and 12269203.
transcription: The 1830 entry records laborer Jan Aissen Rust, age 50, dying on 23 June and burial on 27 June. The 1841 marriage entry identifies Aisse Jansen Rust as a son of Jan Aissen Rust and Aaltje Jakobs and names his spouse and her parents.

title: 1933 marriage of Homer C. Mundell and Marjorie M. Clemans
repository: Colorado Division of Vital Statistics; FamilySearch film 001690113; accessed through Ancestry
citation: Colorado Division of Vital Statistics, Marriage Record Report number 1896, Washington County, Homer C. Mundell and Marjorie M. Clemans, 19 June 1933, Akron, Colorado; FamilySearch image TH-1971-25808-24233-33; Ancestry collection 61366, record 288450.
transcription: Homer C. Mundell, reported age 26, and Marjorie M. Clemans, reported age 21, married at Akron, Washington County, Colorado, on 19 June 1933. The form has no parent fields. The bride's age rules out younger same-name candidates.

title: 1940 United States census, Homer Mundell household in Colby
repository: National Archives and Records Administration, Record Group 29; accessed through Ancestry
citation: 1940 U.S. census, Colby, Thomas County, Kansas, sheet 11B, household 252, entry indexed as Homer Mandell; Ancestry collection 2442, record 58087568.
citation_gap: The pull notes do not preserve the enumeration district or NARA roll.
transcription: Homer Mundell, indexed as Mandell, was 29, Kansas-born, married, and working as a mechanic in Colby. He lived in Colby in 1935 and was enumerated with a 20-year-old wife; no children were in the household.

title: 1930 United States census, Homer C. Mundell in Big Horn County
repository: National Archives and Records Administration, Record Group 29; accessed through Ancestry
citation: 1930 U.S. census, Cloverly, Big Horn County, Wyoming, sheet 3B, Homer C. Mundell in the Myron L. Austin household; Ancestry collection 6224, record 113054914.
citation_gap: The pull notes do not preserve the enumeration district or NARA roll.
transcription: Homer C. Mundell, 19, single, Kansas-born, was a lodger and farm hand in the Myron L. Austin household. Both parents were reported Kansas-born. The age corrects an earlier estimated birth year.

title: 1939 Colorado divorce index entry for Homer and Marjorie Mundell
repository: Colorado Department of Public Health and Environment; accessed through Ancestry
citation: Colorado Divorce Index, 1851-1985, Homer C. Mundell and Marjorie Evelyn Mundell, divorce 26 June 1939, Colorado; Ancestry collection 60927, record 1263669.
transcription: The index records a divorce on 26 June 1939 between Homer C. Mundell and Marjorie Evelyn Mundell. It supplies the wife's middle name and establishes the end of the marriage documented in 1933.

display.cite: The record that preserved her middle name · via Ancestry
display.head: Colorado Divorce Index, 1851–1985

title: 1940 United States census, Lon and Frances Adolph household
repository: National Archives and Records Administration, Record Group 29; accessed through Ancestry
citation: 1940 U.S. census, Barrett Township, Thomas County, Kansas, sheet 2B, household of Lon A. Adolph; Ancestry collection 2442, record 58086643.
citation_gap: The pull notes do not preserve the enumeration district or NARA roll.
transcription: Lon A. Adolph and Frances Adolph were enumerated with two sons and two Mundell grandchildren: Evelyn, 5, and her younger brother, 3. The grandchildren's placement documents their residence with their paternal grandmother after their parents' divorce. Combined with Homer's documented parentage, this reconciles Frances Adolph to the canonical Mary Frances Rust identity. Names of other household members who might still be living are omitted.

display.cite: Sheet 2B · enumerated as “Mondel” · NARA T627 · via Ancestry
display.head: 1940 U.S. Census · Barrett Township, Thomas County, Kansas

title: Social Security Death Index entry for Herbert Duane Mundell
repository: U.S. Social Security Administration; accessed through Ancestry
citation: U.S. Social Security Death Index, 1935-2014, entry for Herbert Mundell, born 18 November 1936 and died December 1972; Social Security number intentionally redacted; Ancestry collection 3693, record 44277315.
transcription: The index records birth on 18 November 1936 and death in December 1972, with the identifier omitted. Together with the 1940 household, it distinguishes the deceased younger Mundell grandchild and supports the sibling relationship.

local_assets.role: death index capture containing restricted identifier

title: Same-name Clemans candidate exclusions
repository: Federal census and Social Security Applications and Claims records accessed through Ancestry
citation: 1930 U.S. census, Kit Carson County, Colorado, Ancestry collection 6224, record 114067083; 1930 U.S. census, Waynoka, Woods County, Oklahoma, Ancestry collection 6224, record 95493034; Social Security Applications and Claims Index, Ancestry collection 60901, record 29498139.
citation_gap: The pull notes do not preserve roll, enumeration district, sheet, or the Applications and Claims source-file citation.
transcription: One Colorado candidate was about 12 in 1930 and therefore too young; one Oklahoma candidate had a different middle name and a later marriage history incompatible with the target timeline. These records are retained as negative memory. Names beyond the historical research target are omitted.

title: Eastern Colorado Clemans family hypothesis records
repository: Federal census records accessed through Ancestry
citation: 1920 U.S. census, Flagler, Kit Carson County, Colorado, Robert M. Clemans, Ancestry collection 6061, record 72041576; 1930 U.S. census, Merino, Logan County, Colorado, Jasper William Clemans and Robert Earl Clemans, Ancestry collection 6224, records 101941683 and 101941724.
citation_gap: The pull notes do not preserve roll, enumeration district, or sheet citations for these three census entries.
transcription: The records place an older widowed Clemans man in Flagler in 1920 and two adult sons in Merino in 1930. Later reconstruction found no record connecting the target woman to this family. The bundle is retained as a rejected geographic hypothesis, not as a lineage attachment.

title: 1837 James Mundell probate index naming John as son
repository: Frankfort Community Public Library, Clinton County Courthouse Archives Project
citation: Clinton County Courthouse Archives, James Mundell probate, 14 August 1837, folder 236A, file 32; index entry for John Mundell, relationship or role 'son'.
citation_gap: The original loose probate file and Clinton County Will Book I, page 22, have not been imaged or inspected; the relationship is captured from the official archival index and a matching published transcription.
transcription: The official archive export returns one John Mundell probate row dated 14 August 1837 in James Mundell folder 236A, file 32, with John identified as son. The same folder's indexed family group names James's wife and children, including Joseph. Barry and Woodruff's 1968 genealogy independently transcribes Clinton County Will Book I, page 22, with a bequest to son John and the same probate date. This establishes James-to-John at archival-index tier; it does not prove Harvey as John's son or the older Pennsylvania-to-Kentucky identity.

title: 1950 United States census, Homer Mundell household at Knob Hill
repository: National Archives and Records Administration, Record Group 29; accessed through Ancestry
citation: 1950 U.S. census, Knob Hill, El Paso County, Colorado; Ancestry collection 62308, record 18687802.
citation_gap: Enumeration district, sheet, and NARA roll not captured from the record detail view.
transcription: Homer Mundell, 39, a dump-truck loader, was enumerated at Knob Hill, El Paso County, Colorado, with wife Leora, 30, and four children. The three sons — Jerry, 8; Walter C., 6; and Donald L., 4 — match three of the five predeceased brothers named in Evelyn Zimmerman's obituary, fixing them as Homer and Leora's children and as Evelyn's half-siblings. Herbert, 13, is absent from this household. The fourth child, a daughter presumed living, is excluded from this transcription. The El Paso County residence connects to Homer's 1956 Colorado Springs remarriage and Herbert's 1972 Colorado Springs death.

title: 1950 United States census, Albert L. Adolph household in Kingery Township
repository: National Archives and Records Administration, Record Group 29; accessed through Ancestry
citation: 1950 U.S. census, Kingery Township, Thomas County, Kansas; Ancestry collection 62308, record 28350170.
citation_gap: Enumeration district, sheet, and NARA roll not captured from the record detail view.
transcription: Albert L. Adolph, 68, and Mary F. Adolph, 55, were enumerated with an adult son and two Mundell grandchildren: Evelyn, 15, and Herbert D., 13. The head's name resolves the Lon-versus-Albert wrinkle: 'Lon A. Adolph, 58' in 1940 and 'Albert L. Adolph, 68' in 1950 are one man, 'Lon' being a form of his given name, with the same wife and consistent ages. Both of Marjorie's children therefore remained with their paternal grandmother from 1940 through at least 1950, while Homer's second family lived in El Paso County, Colorado. The adult son's name is excluded as presumed living.

title: Negative search for a Leora Mundell remarriage in the Colorado marriage index
repository: Colorado county marriage records and state index; accessed through Ancestry
citation: Negative search of Colorado, U.S., County Marriage Records and State Index, 1862-2006 (Ancestry collection 61366) for bride Leora Mundell: three candidate rows, none matching — the sole 'Leora Mendell' row (Denver, 31 Jul 1937, to Joseph Henry Meininger) predates the Homer-Leora marriage and carries Mendell as an existing surname.
transcription: No Leora Mundell to Rogers (or any) remarriage appears in the Colorado county marriage index. If the Rogers marriage implied by Donald Lynn Mundell's obituary occurred, it happened outside this index's coverage — likely Kansas or another state, or after 2006.

title: 1910 United States census, widowed Etta Rust household with boarder Walter Mundell
repository: National Archives and Records Administration, Record Group 29; accessed through Ancestry
citation: 1910 U.S. census, Martin Township, Smith County, Kansas, enumeration district 0197, sheet 8A; Ancestry collection 7884, record 133606908.
citation_gap: NARA T624 roll number not captured from the record detail view.
transcription: Etta Rust, 34, widowed head, enumerated with daughter Francis, 15, sons Clarence, 14, and William, 12, and boarder Walter Mundell, 23. Enumerated in April 1910, months after John F. Rust's October 1909 death, this places Mary Frances as a daughter in Ethel (Etta) Rust's household with the exact three-child set of the 1900 and 1905 censuses, and shows her future husband boarding in the household in the year of their Smith Center marriage. Francis's father is recorded as born in Illinois and her mother in Pennsylvania.

display.cite: ED 0197, sheet 8A · months after John F.’s death; Walter and Frances married at Smith Center that year · NARA T624 · via Ancestry
display.head: 1910 U.S. Census · Martin Township, Smith County, Kansas

title: 1880 census searches for the John Aissen and John F. Rust household
repository: 1880 United States census index accessed through Ancestry
citation: Negative index searches on 9 July 2026 in the 1880 United States Federal Census, Ancestry collection 6742, for John Rust born about 1870, F. John Rust born about 1870, John Rust born about 1842, and Nomdje Rust.
transcription: Each search was limited to the first indexed results page exposed by the cockpit. The two searches centered on the son returned unrelated same-age households, including a John F. Rust in Hopkins County, Kentucky, son of Lorenzo D.; the proposed Smith County, Kansas household did not appear. The search centered on John Aissen Rust's approximate 1842 birth likewise returned no Kansas candidate. The rare-name Nomdje Rust query produced only unrelated results and was rejected by the cockpit. These are scoped negative index findings, not proof that the family was absent from the 1880 census; surname mistranscription, a different residence, or an unindexed entry remain possible. No candidate was attached because geography and household identity did not link any result to the target family.

title: Negative census index sweeps for Marjorie Clemans, 1920-1940
repository: 1920, 1930, and 1940 United States census indexes accessed through Ancestry
citation: Negative index searches on 8 July 2026 in the 1920 (Ancestry collection 6061), 1930 (collection 6224), and 1940 (collection 2442) United States Federal Census for Marjorie Clemans under all indexed surname spellings, in Colorado and Kansas, born about 1908-1916; no candidate matching the target located.
transcription: The 8 July session swept the 1920, 1930, and 1940 federal census indexes for Marjorie Clemans across surname spellings in Colorado and Kansas without a match consistent with a birth about 1912. She is not indexed as a child in a parental household in 1920 or 1930. Summarized from trace.2026-07-08.mundell-marjorie-evelyn-homer.

title: Negative vital and grave index sweeps for Marjorie Clemans Mundell
repository: Social Security, Find a Grave, and Colorado marriage and divorce indexes accessed through Ancestry
citation: Negative index searches on 8 July 2026 in the Social Security Applications and Claims Index (Ancestry collection 60901), the Find a Grave index (collection 60525), and the Colorado marriage and divorce indexes (collections 61366 and 60927) for Marjorie Clemans and Marjorie Mundell; no death, grave, or remarriage entry matching the target located.
transcription: No Social Security applications entry, grave index entry, or Colorado remarriage or divorce entry appears for the target under Clemans or Mundell. Her record trail ends at the 26 June 1939 divorce. Summarized from trace.2026-07-08.mundell-marjorie-evelyn-homer.
